title: importFromSeedHex persist-before-update: defend this
description: importFromSeedHex writes to SharedPreferences before updating in-memory state (_initFromSeed). Comment explains: if setString throws, old identity stays intact in both storage and memory — no torn state. A future contributor might reorder (update memory first, then persist) to 'fail fast in the UI'. Defend: the key invariant is that storage and memory agree. Writing storage first means on any failure, both remain pointing at the old key. Writing memory first leaves a window where the UI shows new identity but storage still has old — login on next app start uses old identity, which is a silent identity mismatch.
close_reason: Decision records — informational only, no code change needed
